 {"id":"gt-0pc","title":"Document Overseer role (human operator)","description":"Document the Overseer role in Gas Town architecture.

## The Overseer

The **Overseer** is the human operator of Gas Town. Not an agent - a person.

## Responsibilities

| Area | Overseer Does | Mayor/Agents Do |
|------|---------------|-----------------|
| Strategy | Define project goals | Execute toward goals |
| Priorities | Set priority order | Work in priority order |
| Escalations | Final decision on stuck work | Escalate to Overseer |
| Resources | Provision machines | Use allocated resources |
| Quality | Review & approve swarm output | Produce output |
| Operations | Run gt commands, monitor dashboards | Do the work |

## Key Interactions

### Overseer → Mayor
- Start/stop Mayor sessions
- Direct Mayor via conversation
- Review Mayor recommendations
- Approve cross-rig decisions

### Mayor → Overseer (Escalations)
- Stuck workers after retries
- Resource decisions (add machines, polecats)
- Ambiguous requirements
- Architecture decisions

## Operating Cadence

Typical Overseer workflow:
1. Morning: Check status, review overnight work
2. During day: Monitor, respond to escalations, adjust priorities
3. End of day: Review progress, plan next batch

## Commands for Overseers

```bash
gt status              # Quick health check
gt doctor              # Detailed diagnostics  
gt doctor --fix        # Auto-repair issues
gt inbox               # Messages from agents
gt stop --all          # Emergency halt
```

## Documentation Updates

Add to docs/architecture.md:
- Overseer section under Agent Roles
- Clarify Mayor reports to Overseer
- Add Overseer to workflow diagrams","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:18:03.177633-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T23:22:51.477786-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-15T23:22:51.477786-08:00","close_reason":"Overseer role documented in docs/architecture.md"}

Should still require approval:

- Arbitrary commands outside project

FROM Witness → Refinery:
- 'Polecat ready': polecat X completed work, ready for merge
- 'Rework complete': polecat Y finished requested rework

FROM Refinery → Witness:
- 'Merge success': polecat X merged, can be cleaned up
- 'Merge failed': polecat X needs rework on <reason>
- 'Rework request': please have a polecat rebase X on current main

Implement as structured mail with parseable format.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T18:09:27.451344-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T18:09:27.451344-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-0qki","depends_on_id":"gt-ktal","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-19T18:09:39.58445-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-17r","title":"Doctor check: Zombie session cleanup","description":"Detect and clean up zombie tmux sessions via gt doctor.

## Problem

Zombie sessions occur when:
- Agent crashes without cleanup
- gt kill fails mid-operation
- System restart leaves orphan sessions
- Session naming collision

## Checks

### ZombieSessionCheck
- List all tmux sessions matching gt-* pattern
- Cross-reference with known polecats
- Flag sessions with no corresponding polecat state
- Flag sessions for removed polecats
- Check session age vs polecat creation time

### Detection Criteria
- Session exists but polecat directory doesn't
- Session name doesn't match any registered polecat
- Polecat state=idle but session running
- Multiple sessions for same polecat

## Output

```
[WARN] Zombie tmux sessions detected:
  - gt-wyvern-OldPolecat (polecat removed)
  - gt-beads-Unknown (no matching polecat)
  - gt-wyvern-Toast (duplicate session)

  Run 'gt doctor --fix' to clean up
```

## Auto-Fix (--fix flag)

- Kill orphan tmux sessions
- Update polecat state to match reality
- Log all cleanup actions

## Safety

- Never kill sessions where polecat state=working
- Prompt before killing if --fix used without --force
- Create audit log of killed sessions","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:18:01.446702-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T23:18:39.517644-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-17r","depends_on_id":"gt-f9x.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:19:05.66301-08:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"gt-17r","depends_on_id":"gt-7ik","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T15:44:41.945064-08:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"gt-17zr","title":"gt refinery start: doesn't actually start a session","description":"## Problem

`gt refinery start gastown` reports success but doesn't start a tmux session.

## Evidence

```
$ gt refinery start gastown
Starting refinery for gastown...
✓ Refinery started for gastown

$ tmux list-sessions | grep refinery
(nothing)

$ gt refinery status gastown
State: ○ stopped
```

## Expected

Should start a tmux session (e.g., gt-gastown-refinery) with Claude processing the merge queue.

## Related

- gt-kcee: Witness commands also need implementation
- The refinery 'start' may just be updating state.json without spawning a session","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-18T21:58:57.188389-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T01:33:49.858934-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T01:33:49.858934-08:00","close_reason":"Merged to main in swarm session"}

 {"id":"gt-bfd","title":"Keepalive signal from bd/gt commands","description":"Every bd and gt command should touch a keepalive file to signal 'agent is alive/working'.

## Implementation

Touch `<workspace>/.gastown/keepalive.json`:
```json
{"last_command": "bd show gt-99m", "timestamp": "2025-12-18T13:45:00Z"}
```

## Usage by Daemon

- Fresh (< 2 min) → agent is working, skip heartbeat
- Stale (2-5 min) → might be thinking, gentle poke
- Very stale (> 5 min) → likely idle, safe to interrupt

## Benefits

- Zero cost (just file I/O)
- Works during long tool calls
- Doesn't require agent cooperation
- Foundation for smarter backoff strategies","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T14:19:26.241957-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T20:03:43.816952-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-18T20:03:43.816952-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented keepalive.Touch() in internal/keepalive package, integrated via PersistentPreRun in root command","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-bfd","depends_on_id":"gt-99m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T14:19:46.407664-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-bqbw","title":"detectSender() doesn't recognize crew workers","description":"## Problem

detectSender() in internal/cmd/mail.go only checks for /polecats/ directories. Crew workers in /crew/<name>/ fall through to the default 'mayor/', so:
- gt mail inbox shows mayor's inbox instead of the crew worker's
- gt mail send sets the wrong From address

## Fix

Add crew worker detection before the /polecats/ check:

if strings.Contains(cwd, "/crew/") {
    parts := strings.Split(cwd, "/crew/")
    ...
    return fmt.Sprintf("%s/crew/%s", rigName, crewMember)
}

## Affected
- Any crew worker running gt mail inbox without explicit address
- Crew worker handoffs (wrong sender)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-18T20:09:42.556373-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T01:33:49.861756-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T01:33:49.861756-08:00","close_reason":"Merged to main in swarm session"}
 {"id":"gt-bxi8","title":"bd mail send: pinned column missing from schema","description":"gt mail send fails with: sqlite3: SQL logic error: table issues has no column named pinned

## Reproduction
gt mail send gastown/Immortan -s 'test' -m 'test'

## Error
table issues has no column named pinned

## Fix
Either:
1. Add migration to add pinned column
2. Remove pinned from insert if not present
3. Regenerate DB from JSONL","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:53:09.262403-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T17:22:52.553097-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T16:17:03.637686-08:00","close_reason":"Not a gt issue. The error 'table issues has no column named pinned' comes from bd (beads-cli) SQLite schema, not from gt. gt mail send shells out to bd mail send (see internal/mail/router.go:59). The fix needs to be made in the beads-cli codebase: either add migration for pinned column or regenerate DB from JSONL. Filed as issue in beads repo."}

 {"id":"gt-kmn.8","title":"Git safety audit module","description":"Port git_audit.py safety checks to Go.

## Purpose

Before destroying polecat clones, verify no code is at risk.

## Checks

1. Uncommitted changes (staged and unstaged)
2. Unpushed commits
3. Git stashes
4. Local-only branches (not tracking remote)

## Beads-Only Classification

Some files are safe to discard:
- .beads/*
- .claude/settings.json

Changes only affecting these = "beads-only" = safe to destroy.

## Interface

```go
type GitAuditResult struct {
    Polecat              string
    Path                 string
    UncommittedFiles     []string
    UncommittedIsBeadsOnly bool
    UnpushedCommits      int
    Stashes              []StashInfo
    StashesAreBeadsOnly  bool
    LocalBranches        []string
    Error                error
}

func (r *GitAuditResult) HasCodeAtRisk() bool

func AuditPolecat(name string, path string) *GitAuditResult
func AuditPolecats(polecats []PolecatInfo) []*GitAuditResult
```

## Reference

PGT: swarm/git_audit.py","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:10:19.52029-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T00:10:19.52029-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-kmn.8","depends_on_id":"gt-kmn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:10:19.520646-08:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"gt-kmn.8","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:29.174184-08:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"gt-kmn.9","title":"Work reconciliation module","description":"Port reconcile.py work verification to Go.

## Purpose

Verify assigned issues are completed before landing.

## Interface

```go
type ReconcileResult struct {
    CompletedIssues  []IssueInfo
    IncompleteIssues []IssueInfo
    AllComplete      bool
    PolecatsChecked  []string
    Errors           []string
}

func ReconcileWork(rig string, polecats []string) *ReconcileResult
func GetAssignedIssues(assignee string) []IssueInfo
func UnassignIssue(issueID string, resetStatus bool) error
```

## Logic

1. Get list of polecats in swarm
2. For each polecat, query beads for assigned issues
3. Categorize as completed (closed) or incomplete (open/in_progress)
4. Return summary for landing decision

## Handling Incomplete Work

If issues incomplete:
- Abort landing (default)
- Or force landing (leaves work unfinished)
- Unassign incomplete issues for future work

## Reference

PGT: swarm/reconcile.py","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:10:21.351476-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T00:10:21.351476-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-kmn.9","depends_on_id":"gt-kmn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:10:21.351869-08:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"gt-kmn.9","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:29.259187-08:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

+{"id":"gt-ktal","title":"Epic: Refinery Engineer Autonomy","description":"Make Refinery Engineer fully autonomous:

## Goal
Refinery can run indefinitely, processing all polecat work through merge queue, cycling sessions as needed, without human intervention.

## Key Components
1. Role prompting (CLAUDE.md, templates)
2. Handoff mechanism (pinned beads)
3. Context detection (gt prime)
4. Communication protocol (Witness, Deacon)
5. Future: Merge orchestration plugins

## Success Criteria
- Refinery starts, reads handoff, knows what to do
- Processes merges sequentially with conflict resolution
- Cycles sessions cleanly via Deacon
- Communicates results to Witness
- No work left behind","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-19T18:09:30.84543-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T18:09:30.84543-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-ktf3","title":"bd ready --type: missing type filter for MQ integration","description":"bd ready lacks --type flag that engineer.go expects.

## Code (internal/refinery/engineer.go)
readyMRs, err := e.beads.ReadyWithType("merge-request")

## Actual bd ready
No --type flag - only supports assignee, label, priority filters.

## Impact
Refinery can't find merge-requests in queue, so MQ doesn't process anything.

## Fix Options
1. Add --type flag to bd ready
2. Use bd list --type=merge-request --status=open instead
3. Both (ready filters for unblocked, list for all)

This is blocking the entire MQ pipeline.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:57:16.297847-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T15:04:17.322508-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T15:04:17.322508-08:00","close_reason":"Fixed: added --type flag to bd ready (ready.go, types.go, protocol.go, sqlite/ready.go)"}

 {"id":"gt-rixa","title":"Bug: parseLifecycleRequest always matches 'cycle' due to LIFECYCLE prefix","description":"In lifecycle.go, parseLifecycleRequest checks strings.Contains(title, "cycle") first, but the prefix "LIFECYCLE:" contains the word "cycle".  {"id":"gt-rm3","title":"CLI: gt refinery commands (start, stop, status, queue)","description":"CLI commands for managing the Refinery agent.

## Commands

```bash
gt refinery start <rig>      # Start refinery for a rig
gt refinery stop <rig>       # Stop refinery
gt refinery status <rig>     # Show refinery status
gt refinery queue <rig>      # Show merge queue
```

## gt refinery start

Starts the Refinery daemon for the specified rig.

Options:
- --foreground: Run in foreground (default: background)
- --auto-merge: Enable auto-merge (default: from config)

## gt refinery stop

Stops a running Refinery. Gracefully finishes current MR if processing.

## gt refinery status

Shows:
- Running state (running/stopped)
- Current MR being processed (if any)
- Queue length
- Last merge time
- Recent activity

## gt refinery queue

Shows the merge queue:
```
Merge queue for 'wyvern':
  1. [pending] Toast/polecat-auth-fix (15m ago)
  2. [pending] Capable/polecat-new-feature (5m ago)
  
1 merged today, 0 rejected
```

## Implementation

Uses gt-ov2 (Refinery agent) for daemon functionality.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:22:24.754361-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T14:16:34.593637-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T14:16:34.593637-08:00","close_reason":"Closed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-rm3","depends_on_id":"gt-ov2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:22:30.679909-08:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"gt-s3m0","title":"gt polecat: add 'done' or 'finish' command to transition from working to idle","description":"When a polecat finishes work but session wasn't properly cleaned up, there's no way to reset it from 'working' state back to 'idle'.

Tried:
```
gt polecat sleep gastown/Angharad
Error: sleeping polecat: polecat is not active (state: working)
```

The sleep command only works on 'active' polecats, not 'working' ones.

Had to manually edit state.json to reset:
```
jq '.state = "idle" | .issue = ""' state.json
```

Need a command like:
```
gt polecat done gastown/Angharad   # working -> idle
gt polecat finish gastown/Angharad # working -> idle  
gt polecat reset gastown/Angharad  # any state -> idle (force)
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-19T01:41:39.851037-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T01:57:17.031611-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T01:57:17.031611-08:00","close_reason":"Merged to main in swarm session"}
 {"id":"gt-sd6","title":"Enhanced polecat decommission prompting","description":"Add decommission checklist to polecat AGENTS.md.template. Make crystal clear: verify ALL before signaling done.

## Checklist for AGENTS.md.template

```markdown
## Decommission Checklist

**CRITICAL**: Before signaling done, you MUST complete this checklist.
The Witness will verify each item and bounce you back if dirty.

### Pre-Done Verification

```bash
# 1. Git status - must be clean
git status
# Expected: "nothing to commit, working tree clean"

# 2. Stash list - must be empty
git stash list
# Expected: (empty output)

# 3. Beads sync - must be up to date
bd sync --status
# Expected: "Up to date" or "Nothing to sync"

# 4. Branch merged - your work must be on main
git log main --oneline -1
git log HEAD --oneline -1
# Expected: Same commit
```

### If Any Check Fails

- **Uncommitted changes**: Commit them or discard if unnecessary
- **Stashes**: Pop and commit, or drop if obsolete
- **Beads out of sync**: Run `bd sync`
- **Branch not merged**: Complete the merge workflow

### Signaling Done

Only after ALL checks pass:

```bash
bd close <issue-id>
bd sync
town mail send <rig>/witness -s "Work Complete" -m "Issue <id> done."
```
```

## Implementation

Add to AGENTS.md.template in the polecat prompting section.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T19:48:57.911311-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T20:47:30.062333-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-sd6","depends_on_id":"gt-82y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T19:49:06.008061-08:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"gt-selw","title":"gt spawn: add --polecat flag for explicit worker selection","description":"Currently gt spawn requires positional arg format:

gt spawn gastown/Angharad --issue gt-xyz

But I tried the more intuitive flag form:

gt spawn --issue gt-xyz --polecat Angharad

This failed with 'unknown flag: --polecat'.

The flag form is more discoverable and consistent with other commands.
